title: Geophila reniformis
intro: Geophila reniformis, also known as creeping Jenny or kidney-leaf geophila, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Geophila reniformis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Geophila reniformis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Geophila reniformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It belongs to the genus Geophila, which is a group of low-growing, herbaceous plants. The plant is native to tropical Africa and is often found in shaded, humid environments. Geophila reniformis is a relatively small plant with oval-shaped leaves and small, white flowers. It is an epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or objects without deriving nutrients from them.
